metadata.description: A-Level Economics topics with prerequisite ordering, colored by component
sources: Cambridge International AS & A Level Economics 9708 Syllabus

nodes.label: Scarcity, Choice & Opportunity Cost
nodes.description: The fundamental economic problem: unlimited wants vs limited resources, necessity of choice, and the concept of opportunity cost as the next best alternative forgone.

nodes.label: Economic Methodology
nodes.description: Positive and normative statements, ceteris paribus, the use of models and assumptions in economics, and the scientific method in economic analysis.

nodes.label: Factors of Production
nodes.description: Land, labour, capital, and enterprise as the four factors of production; factor rewards (rent, wages, interest, profit); factor mobility and its economic significance.

nodes.label: Resource Allocation in Economic Systems
nodes.description: How market economies, command economies, and mixed economies allocate resources; the roles of the price mechanism, planning authorities, and government in resource allocation.

nodes.label: Production Possibility Curve
nodes.description: The PPC as a model of scarcity and choice; shapes and shifts of the PPC; opportunity cost illustrated on the PPC; points inside, on, and outside the frontier.

nodes.label: Demand
nodes.description: The law of demand, individual and market demand curves, movements along vs shifts of the demand curve, and the conditions of demand (income, substitutes, complements, tastes, expectations).

nodes.label: Supply
nodes.description: The law of supply, individual and market supply curves, movements along vs shifts of the supply curve, and the conditions of supply (costs, technology, taxes, subsidies, expectations).

nodes.label: Price Determination
nodes.description: Market equilibrium where demand equals supply; how changes in demand and supply shift equilibrium price and quantity; the signalling, incentive, and rationing functions of the price mechanism.

nodes.label: Consumer & Producer Surplus
nodes.description: Consumer surplus as the difference between willingness to pay and market price; producer surplus as the difference between market price and minimum acceptable price; total welfare and deadweight loss.

nodes.label: Price Elasticity of Demand
nodes.description: PED as a measure of responsiveness of quantity demanded to price changes; calculation, interpretation, determinants; elastic, inelastic, unit elastic, perfectly elastic and perfectly inelastic demand; PED and total revenue.

nodes.label: Income Elasticity of Demand
nodes.description: YED as a measure of responsiveness of demand to income changes; calculation and interpretation; normal goods vs inferior goods; necessities vs luxuries; significance for businesses and the economy.

nodes.label: Cross Elasticity of Demand
nodes.description: XED as a measure of responsiveness of demand for one good to a change in price of another; calculation and interpretation; substitutes (positive XED) vs complements (negative XED); business significance.

nodes.label: Price Elasticity of Supply
nodes.description: PES as a measure of responsiveness of quantity supplied to price changes; calculation, interpretation, determinants including time, spare capacity, stocks, and factor mobility.

nodes.label: Market Failure
nodes.description: When markets fail to allocate resources efficiently; types of market failure including externalities, public goods, merit/demerit goods, information failure, and market power; the concept of misallocation of resources.

nodes.label: Externalities
nodes.description: Positive and negative externalities in production and consumption; private costs/benefits vs social costs/benefits; marginal analysis of externalities; welfare loss from externalities shown on diagrams.

nodes.label: Public Goods
nodes.description: Characteristics of public goods: non-rivalry and non-excludability; the free-rider problem; why markets fail to provide public goods; quasi-public goods; government provision of public goods.

nodes.label: Merit & Demerit Goods
nodes.description: Merit goods as goods under-consumed due to information failure and positive externalities; demerit goods as goods over-consumed; the role of value judgements; government policies to correct consumption levels.

nodes.label: Information Failure
nodes.description: Symmetric vs asymmetric information; how incomplete or inaccurate information leads to market failure; moral hazard and adverse selection; the impact on consumer and producer decision-making.

nodes.label: Government Microeconomic Intervention
nodes.description: Indirect taxes and subsidies to correct externalities; maximum and minimum prices; buffer stock schemes; regulation and legislation; tradable pollution permits; direct provision of goods and services.

nodes.label: Government Failure
nodes.description: When government intervention leads to a net welfare loss; causes including distortion of price signals, unintended consequences, administrative costs, information gaps, and political self-interest.

nodes.label: National Income
nodes.description: Measuring economic activity using GDP, GNP, and NNP; the circular flow of income model; injections and withdrawals; the three methods of measuring national income (output, income, expenditure).

nodes.label: Circular Flow of Income
nodes.description: The two-sector, three-sector, and four-sector circular flow models; injections (investment, government spending, exports) and withdrawals (savings, taxation, imports); equilibrium in the circular flow.

nodes.label: Aggregate Demand
nodes.description: The components of AD (C + I + G + X - M); the AD curve and reasons for its downward slope (wealth effect, interest rate effect, trade effect); factors causing shifts in AD.

nodes.label: Consumption & Savings
nodes.description: The consumption function; average and marginal propensity to consume and save; determinants of consumption and savings (income, interest rates, wealth, confidence, availability of credit).

nodes.label: Investment
nodes.description: Gross and net investment; determinants of investment (interest rates, business confidence, expected profits, government policy, technology); the accelerator effect; the role of investment in the economy.

nodes.label: Aggregate Supply
nodes.description: Short-run AS and long-run AS; the shape of the AS curve in Keynesian and classical/monetarist models; factors causing shifts in SRAS and LRAS; macroeconomic equilibrium using AD/AS.

nodes.label: Macroeconomic Equilibrium
nodes.description: Determination of the price level and real output through AD/AS interaction; short-run and long-run equilibrium; the effects of demand-side and supply-side shocks on equilibrium.

nodes.label: Inflation
nodes.description: Definition and measurement of inflation (CPI, RPI); causes of inflation (demand-pull, cost-push); consequences of inflation for consumers, firms, government, and the economy; hyperinflation and deflation.

nodes.label: Deflation & Disinflation
nodes.description: The distinction between deflation and disinflation; causes of deflation (demand-side and supply-side); consequences of deflation including the deflationary spiral; policy responses to deflation.

nodes.label: Unemployment
nodes.description: Definition and measurement of unemployment; types of unemployment (cyclical, structural, frictional, seasonal); causes and consequences of unemployment; the natural rate of unemployment.

nodes.label: Balance of Payments
nodes.description: Structure of the balance of payments: current account, capital account, and financial account; causes and consequences of current account deficits and surpluses; policies to correct imbalances.

nodes.label: Exchange Rates
nodes.description: Floating, fixed, and managed exchange rate systems; determination of exchange rates by supply and demand for currency; causes and consequences of exchange rate changes; impact on the current account.

nodes.label: Terms of Trade
nodes.description: Definition and calculation of the terms of trade; causes of changes in terms of trade; the impact of changes in the terms of trade on the balance of payments and living standards.

nodes.label: Monetary Policy
nodes.description: The role of the central bank; instruments of monetary policy (interest rates, money supply, quantitative easing); the transmission mechanism; effectiveness and limitations of monetary policy.

nodes.label: Fiscal Policy
nodes.description: Government revenue and expenditure; direct and indirect taxation; budget deficits and surpluses; national debt; the use of fiscal policy to manage aggregate demand; automatic stabilisers.

nodes.label: Supply-Side Policies
nodes.description: Market-based supply-side policies (deregulation, privatisation, tax reform, labour market flexibility) and interventionist supply-side policies (education, infrastructure, industrial policy); evaluation of their effectiveness.

nodes.label: Money & Banking
nodes.description: Functions of money (medium of exchange, store of value, unit of account, standard of deferred payment); characteristics of money; the role of commercial banks and the central bank; credit creation and the money multiplier.

nodes.label: Policy Objectives & Conflicts
nodes.description: The four main macroeconomic objectives (growth, low inflation, low unemployment, balance of payments equilibrium); conflicts between objectives; the trade-off between inflation and unemployment.

nodes.label: Law of Diminishing Returns
nodes.description: Short-run production theory; total, average, and marginal product; the law of diminishing marginal returns; the relationship between marginal and average product; fixed and variable factors.

nodes.label: Short-Run Costs
nodes.description: Fixed costs, variable costs, total costs; average fixed cost, average variable cost, average total cost, and marginal cost; the relationship between marginal cost and average cost curves; the link between production and cost curves.

nodes.label: Long-Run Costs & Economies of Scale
nodes.description: Long-run average cost curve; internal economies of scale (technical, managerial, financial, marketing, risk-bearing); internal diseconomies of scale; external economies and diseconomies of scale; minimum efficient scale.

nodes.label: Revenue
nodes.description: Total revenue, average revenue, and marginal revenue; the relationship between AR, MR, and demand; revenue curves for price-taking and price-setting firms; the relationship between elasticity and revenue.

nodes.label: Profit
nodes.description: Normal profit vs supernormal (abnormal) profit; the role of profit in a market economy; profit maximisation (MC = MR); alternative objectives of firms (revenue maximisation, sales maximisation, satisficing).

nodes.label: Perfect Competition
nodes.description: Assumptions of perfect competition; short-run equilibrium (supernormal profit, normal profit, losses); long-run equilibrium and the adjustment process; efficiency in perfect competition (allocative and productive).

nodes.label: Monopoly
nodes.description: Sources of monopoly power and barriers to entry; short-run and long-run equilibrium under monopoly; price and output determination; comparison with perfect competition; natural monopoly.

nodes.label: Price Discrimination
nodes.description: First, second, and third degree price discrimination; conditions necessary for price discrimination; advantages and disadvantages for consumers and producers; diagrammatic analysis.

nodes.label: Monopolistic Competition
nodes.description: Assumptions and characteristics; product differentiation and non-price competition; short-run and long-run equilibrium; comparison with perfect competition and monopoly; efficiency implications.

nodes.label: Oligopoly
nodes.description: Characteristics of oligopoly; interdependence and uncertainty; the kinked demand curve model; collusive and non-collusive behaviour; cartels; game theory and the prisoner's dilemma; concentration ratios.

nodes.label: Contestable Markets
nodes.description: The theory of contestable markets; conditions for contestability (low barriers to entry and exit, low sunk costs); hit-and-run competition; implications for pricing and efficiency; relevance to real-world markets.

nodes.label: Growth of Firms
nodes.description: Internal (organic) growth vs external growth; mergers and takeovers (horizontal, vertical, conglomerate); reasons for growth; the principal-agent problem; separation of ownership and control; small firms.

nodes.label: Objectives of Firms
nodes.description: Profit maximisation; revenue maximisation; sales maximisation; satisficing; corporate social responsibility; managerial and behavioural theories of the firm; the impact of different objectives on price and output.

nodes.label: Allocative Efficiency
nodes.description: Allocative efficiency where price equals marginal cost (P = MC); Pareto optimality; how different market structures achieve or fail to achieve allocative efficiency; welfare implications.

nodes.label: Productive Efficiency
nodes.description: Productive efficiency where production occurs at minimum average cost; the relationship between productive efficiency and market structure; comparison of efficiency across market structures.

nodes.label: Dynamic Efficiency
nodes.description: Dynamic efficiency through innovation and technological progress over time; the Schumpeterian hypothesis that monopoly power promotes dynamic efficiency; creative destruction; R&D investment.

nodes.label: X-Inefficiency
nodes.description: X-inefficiency as the failure to minimise costs due to lack of competitive pressure; the relationship between market power and X-inefficiency; implications for monopoly and public sector organisations.

nodes.label: Demand for Labour
nodes.description: Derived demand for labour; marginal revenue product theory (MRP = MPP x MR); factors affecting demand for labour; the firm's demand curve for labour; shifts in demand for labour.

nodes.label: Supply of Labour
nodes.description: Individual labour supply and the backward-bending supply curve; income and substitution effects of wage changes; factors affecting the supply of labour to an occupation and to the economy; net migration.

nodes.label: Wage Determination
nodes.description: Competitive labour markets and wage determination by supply and demand; factors causing wage differentials between occupations and individuals; human capital theory; compensating wage differentials.

nodes.label: Monopsony in Labour Markets
nodes.description: Monopsony power in labour markets; wage and employment determination under monopsony; comparison with competitive labour markets; the impact on wages and employment levels.

nodes.label: Trade Unions
nodes.description: The role and functions of trade unions; trade union objectives and methods; the impact of trade unions on wages and employment; bilateral monopoly in labour markets; collective bargaining.

nodes.label: Minimum Wage & Labour Market Intervention
nodes.description: National minimum wage and its effects on employment, wages, and poverty; maximum wage policies; government training and education policies; anti-discrimination legislation in labour markets.

nodes.label: Comparison of Market Structures
nodes.description: Comparing price, output, profit, and efficiency across perfect competition, monopolistic competition, oligopoly, and monopoly; the spectrum of competition; implications for consumers and producers.

nodes.label: Competition Policy
nodes.description: Government policies to promote competition; regulation of monopolies and mergers; anti-competitive practices; the role of competition authorities; privatisation, deregulation, and nationalisation.

nodes.label: Income & Wealth Inequality
nodes.description: Measurement of inequality using Lorenz curves and Gini coefficients; causes of income and wealth inequality; the distinction between equity and equality; policies to redistribute income and wealth.

nodes.label: Economic Growth
nodes.description: Actual growth vs potential growth; short-run and long-run growth; the causes of economic growth (factors of production, productivity, technology); costs and benefits of economic growth; the output gap.

nodes.label: Sustainability & the Environment
nodes.description: Sustainable development; the conflict between growth and environmental protection; renewable and non-renewable resources; climate change and economic policy; green GDP; intergenerational equity.

nodes.label: National Income Statistics
nodes.description: Uses and limitations of national income data; real vs nominal GDP; GDP per capita; purchasing power parity; the informal economy; limitations of GDP as a measure of welfare and living standards.

nodes.label: Keynesian Multiplier
nodes.description: The multiplier effect; the relationship between MPC, MPS, MPT, MPM and the size of the multiplier; calculation of the multiplier; the multiplier process and its implications for fiscal policy.

nodes.label: Accelerator Theory
nodes.description: The accelerator principle and the relationship between changes in national income and the level of investment; interaction between the multiplier and accelerator; implications for economic cycles.

nodes.label: The Business Cycle
nodes.description: Phases of the business cycle (boom, recession, slump, recovery); causes of cyclical fluctuations; the output gap; the role of the multiplier-accelerator interaction; implications for government policy.

nodes.label: Money, Banking & Monetary Policy (A2)
nodes.description: The money supply and its determinants; the quantity theory of money; the role of the central bank in controlling money supply and inflation; the monetary transmission mechanism; quantitative easing.

nodes.label: Phillips Curve
nodes.description: The original Phillips curve relationship between unemployment and wage inflation; the short-run and long-run Phillips curve; the expectations-augmented Phillips curve; the NAIRU; policy implications.

nodes.label: Keynesian vs Monetarist Debate
nodes.description: Keynesian economics and the role of aggregate demand management; monetarist view emphasising money supply control; differing views on the shape of AS, the role of fiscal policy, and the effectiveness of government intervention.

nodes.label: Fiscal Policy Evaluation (A2)
nodes.description: Evaluation of fiscal policy effectiveness; automatic stabilisers vs discretionary policy; the Laffer curve; crowding out; time lags; structural vs cyclical budget deficits; national debt sustainability.

nodes.label: Supply-Side Policies Evaluation (A2)
nodes.description: A2-level evaluation of supply-side policies; effectiveness in promoting long-run growth; the trade-off between equity and efficiency; time lags and limitations; free-market vs interventionist approaches.

nodes.label: International Trade
nodes.description: Absolute and comparative advantage; the gains from trade; free trade vs protectionism; tariffs, quotas, and subsidies; the WTO; trading blocs and economic integration; terms of trade.

nodes.label: Protectionism
nodes.description: Arguments for and against protectionism; types of protectionist measures (tariffs, quotas, subsidies, non-tariff barriers); the infant industry argument; the impact of protectionism on welfare.

nodes.label: Exchange Rates (A2)
nodes.description: Advanced analysis of exchange rate determination; the Marshall-Lerner condition and the J-curve effect; purchasing power parity; exchange rate policy and its impact on macroeconomic performance.

nodes.label: Balance of Payments (A2)
nodes.description: Advanced analysis of balance of payments; causes of persistent current account deficits and surpluses; expenditure-switching and expenditure-reducing policies; evaluation of corrective policies.

nodes.label: Globalisation
nodes.description: Causes and consequences of globalisation; multinational corporations and foreign direct investment; the impact on developed and developing economies; trade liberalisation and its effects.

nodes.label: Development Economics
nodes.description: The distinction between economic growth and economic development; indicators of development (HDI, MPI); characteristics of developing economies; the importance of development as a policy objective.

nodes.label: Barriers to Development
nodes.description: Poverty traps and the poverty cycle; institutional barriers (corruption, property rights); primary product dependency; savings gap; debt; human capital deficiency; capital flight; political instability.

nodes.label: Strategies for Development
nodes.description: Aid (bilateral, multilateral, tied); trade liberalisation; foreign direct investment; microfinance; debt relief; industrialisation and export-led growth; import substitution; the role of international institutions (IMF, World Bank).

nodes.label: Inequality & Poverty
nodes.description: Absolute vs relative poverty; causes of poverty in developing and developed economies; the relationship between growth and inequality; policies to reduce poverty and inequality; the Kuznets curve.

nodes.label: Government Macroeconomic Intervention
nodes.description: The role of government in managing the macroeconomy; policy instruments and their effectiveness; the debate between interventionist and free-market approaches; policy evaluation and trade-offs.
